old_claim.note: Implicit generated context did not explicitly model canal or waterway infrastructure as the direct dependency.

new_claim.note: A canal lock is a gated chamber in a canal or navigable waterway, so canal/waterway infrastructure is a direct scope dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: Definition describing a lock as a gated basin in the course of a canal or river.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source defines locks as canal or river infrastructure with gates at each end for changing boat elevation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the scoped node is canal lock gates, the canal or navigable-waterway infrastructure is part of the definition rather than optional context.

ontology_before: Canal lock gates were modeled through municipal water supply, guilds, and rudders without the canal infrastructure dependency.
ontology_after: Canal lock gates directly depend on canal networks as the waterway infrastructure they modify.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: canal_lock_gates -> canal_networks is required for the scoped navigation-lock technology.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to generic sluice gates or valves outside navigation canals.
A future split separated generic water gates from canal locks and moved this edge only to the canal-lock node.
The edge note implied all canal networks require locks rather than locks requiring a canal or navigable waterway.
short_reason: A canal lock is defined as lock infrastructure in a canal or navigable waterway.
